title: Fermented Indulgent Brazilian Bread Flour Gratin
description: A rich, molten Brazilian-style gratin featuring fermented bread flour and a touch of spice. Perfect for a indulgent dinner or dessert.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine bread flour, water, sugar substitute, yeast, and salt. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it ferment in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and roll it out into a 1/4-inch thick circle. Transfer the dough to a greased 9x13-inch baking dish.
Mix the butter, egg yolks, Gruy0re cheese, piment8on flakes, and black pepper in a bowl until well combined.
Spread the cheese mixture evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Bake the gratin for 20-25 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is still slightly jiggly.
Remove the gratin from the oven and immediately transfer it to an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Once cooled, refrigerate the gratin for at least 2 hours before serving.
